mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in months with PAYEMS > 100K monthly gain are negative in > 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% and has risen by at least 25 basis points over the prior 3 months, monetary tightening reduces equity valuations via higher discount rates and lower risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the trigger signal over 60-day forward returns remains above -0.03 for any 3-year window, or the proportion of negative forward returns in triggered instances is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals broad-based economic strength with balanced demand and supply, which improves corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and INDPRO 6-month moving average above their respective 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return in triggered months is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 2% signals diminishing economic momentum and rising recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: During late-cycle regimes (as defined by yield curve inversion or peak PMI), triggered months do not show statistically significant negative forward returns (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation reduces the real value of future cash flows and often prompts monetary tightening, both of which are negative for equity prices; a sustained upward trend in CPIAUCSL therefore predicts negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of CPIAUCSL month-over-month change against 20-day forward returns is greater than 0.03 or negative in less than 60% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4% indicates an extremely tight labor market that historically precedes wage-driven inflation and eventual Fed tightening, which causes near-term weakness in equity markets over the upcoming week.
failure_condition: Over any 10-year window, the average 5-day forward return in months when UNRATE is below 4% is not significantly less than the unconditional mean (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is declining while the federal funds rate is rising, the economy faces simultaneous supply weakness and monetary tightening, a stagflationary combination that strongly depresses S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO < 0% and 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0%
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal (INDPRO decline & FEDFUNDS rise) against 60-day forward returns is above -0.05 or the proportion of negative returns in triggered instances is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery phase, strong quarterly GDP growth (annualized rate above 2%) confirms the expansion is self-sustaining, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: During NBER-defined recoveries, the average 60-day forward return following GDP releases with growth > 2% is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate is below 55%.
